title: cassia cowanii var. guianensis
intro: Cassia cowanii var. guianensis, also known as the Guianan Senna, is a variety of plant in the Fabaceae family that is native to the Guianas and other parts of South America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative and for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cassia cowanii var. guianensis include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, among others. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and pods can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of health problems.
